Acción Serrana

Acción Serrana focuses on biocultural restoration, reforestation, community organizing, and building long-term ecological and cultural resilience in mountainous regions as part of the larger Acción An

33314415-10e1-81e9-8128-ea0ce6f4ef5b, 33314415-10e1-8166-8f9f-dece6becb656, 33314415-10e1-81b4-970f-c160b60033dd, 33314415-10e1-81cc-a020-d67107a57549, 33314415-10e1-812b-acfc-d3fee02a54e6, 33314415-10e1-8130-a7c8-fd799742f0fd, 33314415-10e1-81c8-a59c-cac958e575ea

type
organization
location_lat
-31.4
background_section
## Background In the heart of Argentina, the Sierras Grandes of Córdoba represent the southernmost extension of the Andean mountain system. This high-altitude landscape shelters the [Polylepis australis](https://youtu.be/zS367PdPNqw) forest—known locally as "tabaquillo"—the highest-growing native trees in the region, forming the highest forest on Earth. These forests are critically important: they protect watershed headwaters, improve soil quality through fallen leaves and root systems, retain soil against erosion, and provide habitat for many endemic species adapted to the harsh mountain environment. Yet these forests face severe threats: overgrazing by cattle and other animals, forest fires, and [accelerating soil erosion.](https://youtu.be/zS367PdPNqw) For generations, this ecosystem has provided water for the province of Córdoba and supported indigenous and gaucho communities who rely on its ecological integrity. The Sierras Grandes encompass 155,000 hectares of provincial water reserve and 37,000 hectares of national park—the Quebrada del Condorito National Park and the Pampa de Achala provincial water reserve—a territory where restoration work must balance ecological recovery with cultural continuity. ### Origins and Network Acción Serrana operates as a fractal of the larger Acción Andina alliance, a large-scale multinational initiative to restore high-altitude Polylepis ecosystems across the Andean region. Founded in 2018, Acción Andina aims to [restore 1 million hectares](](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YPJ2Thbgbdc&ab_channel=TheBioFiProject)) over the next 25 years. This network operates across six countries: Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia, Colombia, Chile, and Argentina. Together, the alliance has planted more than 12.5 million trees, representing a coordinated effort to restore ecosystems that are critical water sources for major South American rivers.

issues with location
description
Acción Serrana focuses on biocultural restoration, reforestation, community organizing, and building long-term ecological and cultural resilience in mountainous regions as part of the larger Acción Andina coalition.
id
accion-serrana
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy By 2025, the organization expects to reach the milestone of [1 million trees planted](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YPJ2Thbgbdc&ab_channel=TheBioFiProject) in the Sierras Grandes. But the deeper impact extends beyond tree counts: the initiative has restored forests where all the water for the province of Córdoba originates, while simultaneously contributing to Acción Andina's work restoring ecosystems that feed major South American rivers. The project aims to offer a replicable approach to high-altitude forest restoration that can inform work across Andean landscapes and beyond.
website
https://www.accionserrana.com/
bioregional_context
The Sierras Grandes of Córdoba are part of the Andes' southernmost reaches, a vital source of freshwater and biodiversity. This region is threatened by deforestation, desertification, and climate change, strongly impacting indigenous and gaucho communities who rely on its ecological health.
category
Bioregional Organizer
